Phelan: If I had the patience and time, I would actually ask a detailed doctrine question, and engage in a back and forth with Han or someone else discussing that, and completely ignore the duck's annoying philosophy discussion.
C[Phelan: Not that I'm that interested in religion, but he's an idiot when religion is even mentioned
Phelan: and I feel like him jumping onto a thread that was created to discuss religion, to basically call religious people jerks and assholes
Phelan: especially when he then insists that forcing morals on others is wrong
Phelan: and in the next phrase, tells Han that han's morals are wrong, and his are right.
JohnnyWarpzone: is right about a lot of things
JohnnyWarpzone: But he presents those opinions in an unclear, aggressive and unhelpful manner
C[JohnnyWarpzone: There's a difference between holding strong opinions on morality, and thinking your views are objectively right
Phelan: even more of a difference between holding strong opinions on morality, and calling others who differ assholes, jerks, etc
Phelan: also, sorry for not replying
Phelan: been playing on the app :)
C[JohnnyWarpzone: Haha, I said all that like 15 minutes ago. :P
JohnnyWarpzone: As I said, I think he's right about a lot of opinions. I don't think he's right about how he treats others for their opinions.
C[JohnnyWarpzone: And I'd rather not have him on my side, considering how he makes my side look in all the discussions he agrees with me.
Phelan: I don't agree with a lot of his opinions. I think he's way too fundamentalist on what he thinks(or says, at least)
C[Phelan: and I guess I'm too empathic
Phelan: Even when I disagree utterly with someone
Phelan: I try to treat them fairly
C[JohnnyWarpzone: You won't see me yell at someone for disagreeing with me

JohnnyWarpzone: I mean. I think that organized religion is a net negative in the modern world. That's a touchy subject! And I'm not going to go around pushing it down peoples' throats because what on earth would be the point of that?
Phelan: I won't say ""net negative"", as I think there's been both a lot of good things and bad things done in the name of religion
JohnnyWarpzone: I think religion has served good in the past and has now moved past the point of being useful to us as a species.
Phelan: but I do think that if you want the people that take religion too far to change, you won't do it by pushing them aggressively, or without empathy
JohnnyWarpzone: Christianity used to be the biggest source of science and knowledge in Europe!
Phelan: well, to be fair, that was during the middle ages
Phelan: and no one else had access to it :p
C[Phelan: but I do agree that most forms of organized religion are starting not to make sense
Phelan: luckily, the intelligent ones are adapting to the changes
Phelan: sadly, some of those intelligent ones can have bad agendas
JohnnyWarpzone: originally the reason nobody else had knowledge was that the pan-european government at the time (western roman empire) collapsed and took all their knowledge with them.
JohnnyWarpzone: The catholic church was the only ones with the organizational capacity to try to retain some of that knowledge
Phelan: (I'm not really a history buff, so I don't know much about the middle ages)
Phelan: and even when I do know about bits, I rarely have them in the right order :p
JohnnyWarpzone: Then eventually the catholic church transformed in order to become a sort of government during the middle ages and at some point they became very jealous about their knowledge.
JohnnyWarpzone: I'm a fan of history. I wouldn't call myself an authoritative source, but I know more about it than most people I know
C[Phelan: I do think that people should know enough about history, or know how to research it, as not knowing can cause repetions of things that might be avoided
Phelan: but I never could memorize things chronologically
JohnnyWarpzone: I think politicians in particular should study both science and history a lot more than they do.
JohnnyWarpzone: Honestly history isn't even about that, and I hated history until I met a history teacher who understood that.
JohnnyWarpzone: History isn't about dates and numbers of casualties, it is mainly about the forces that cause change to happen
JohnnyWarpzone: wars are part of that, and specific battles canbe important, but they are not *history*
C[JohnnyWarpzone: Or more specifically, they are not the entirety of history
Phelan: but to get the cause-effect thing right, you do need to have things in order
C[JohnnyWarpzone: It's hard to when you have no interest. Which was why I did horribly in history for most of my school years :p
